titleStmt.title: SII 9.284: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 284. (A.R. No. 346 of 1905.) ON A HERO-STONE SET UP ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE TALAKANTAMMA TEMPLE AT DEVAGUDI, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated the cyclic year Śukla, Jyēshṭha, amāvāsye (not verifiable). It mentions the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhīmasēna who seems to have been a general of (the Chāḷukya king ?) Trailōkyamalla. The general was a devotee of the goddess Taḍalakuṭīdēvī. A hero named Kētaya is said to have fought with Kaṭakada-Chandradaṇḍanāyaka and died after having slain two horses and a cavalier.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmat sukula
saṁvascarada jēṣṭamāsada Amāvāsyeyādivāra
svasti samastaprasastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāma(ṁ)ṇḍaḷēsvaraṁ
traiḷōkyamallabhujabaḷavīraṁ Asahāyavīraṁ dāyādyadallaḷaṁ
śrītaḍalakuṭīdēvīvaraprasādōdubhavaṁ bhīmasēna
dēviA divyaśrīpādapadmaṁgaḷige trisaṁjeyāḷaṁ śāṣṭāṁgameṟa
gi poḍevaṭṭu karaṁgaḷaṁ mugiyalu prasa(ṁ)nnavādaḷ nosalaka(ṁ)ṇnamūrtti
bhīmarasana taṁtrapāḷa toṁḍeyana bhāva kētayana kuṟumaṟi Apasubā
ḷa holamēreya sīmakke kaṭakada caṁdradaṁḍanāyakana ku
dureA mēlevaridaḍe haṟategeyade Eraḍkudu
reAneccikaṭṭinalaginalu yoṁdkudu
reyaṁ rāhutanaṁ
koṁdu svarggastanādaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 9.284 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
